text: and then reach raised up to flap and reach up to the step muscles.

Separate the step muscles and sternothyroid and sternohyoid muscles and then open the sternothyroid muscles from the thyroid module and dissect from the lower pole of the thyroid and identify the recurrent laryngeal nerve and parathyroids.
Then cut the isthmus and finally reach up to the upper pole and dissect it.
So you raise the flap, you see the midline and step muscles and sternocleidomastoid muscles are clearly visible.
and then separate the sternocleidomastoid muscles from the stroke muscles and then open the midline.

Once you open the midline, you dissect it out from lateral side and clearly identify the recurrent laryngeal nerve and then dissect it out from the recurrent laryngeal nerve and save the parathyroid and nerve both.
And take it out by specimen, close the midline.

and then endotracheal intubation sometimes we require nasal intubation also and then giving the incision in the lower lip in the midline and then infiltrate with the dendrosaline solution and then put all three ports once all three ports are putted this is the position and then we raise the slab from upside down then open the midline we cut the stomach and then dissect the stuck muscles over the

thyroid lobe and then anchor the strep muscles from anchor structures and then create the medial space and identify the superior polar vessels and then handle it and then dissect laterally to identify the recurrent lesion nerve and parathyroid and finally remove the specimen through that same incision and then check for hemostasis, close the midline and then suture the opening port side.
